name: DecimalChar

values.name: isBasicLatin
values.comment:
Check if a character is the Basic Latin digit (i.e., between 0x0030 and 0x0039 in code points).

    isBasicLatin '3'
    --> True

    isBasicLatin '３'
    --> False

values.name: isDecimal
values.comment:
Check if a character is decimal (i.e., `Numeric_Type=Decimal`).

    isDecimal '3'
    --> True

    isDecimal '３'
    --> True

    isDecimal '🐐'
    --> False

    -- Roman digit is numeric, but not decimal.
    isDecimal 'Ⅵ'
    --> False

values.name: normalizeAsDigits
values.comment:
Extract only the decimal characters contained in a given string, and convert them to the corresponding Basic Latin characters.

    normalizeAsDigits "０１２３🐐４５٩🌱"
    --> "0123459"

values.name: normalizeAsText
values.comment:
Similar to `normalizeAsDigits`, but `normalizeAsText` leaves non-decimal characters as they are.

    normalizeAsText "０１２３🐐４５٩🌱"
    --> "0123🐐459🌱"

values.name: toBasicLatin
values.comment:
Convert a character to the corresponding Basic Latin digit character.

If the character is not decimal, it returns `Nothing`.

    toBasicLatin '３'
    --> Just '3'

    -- Arabic numeral representing '9'
    toBasicLatin '٩'
    --> Just '9'

    toBasicLatin '🐐'
    --> Nothing
